label: Charterhouse Lodgings
description: Charterhouse Lodgings occupies a narrow, two-storey rowhouse of blackened brick pinned with riveted iron straps, its ground floor set behind a barred vestibule and a licensable threshold-stone. The interior is divided into small chambers with soot-stained plaster, built-in cupboards, and a single hearth whose flue is threaded through older, broken masonry from the city’s refounding. A wall niche holds an iron-bound document coffer and a recessed shelf for seal-wax and cord, reflecting Cindervale’s culture of charters, tolls, and sworn record. Shuttered slit-windows and a steep stairwell emphasize defensibility and privacy in a city governed by quarantine ordinances and interdiction law.
